title: Ship agent-monitor daemon (steez-owned agent-state, no vendoring)
description: Replans the dot-ktn chain. Original plan vendored agent-state into dotfiles for reproducibility; decision: that was wrong because agent-state is actively evolving in steez and two copies inevitably drift. New plan keeps one agent-state — the live one in ~/.steez/repo/shared/steez/bin/agent-state — and ships only the Python monitor daemon from dotfiles. Daemon already resolves agent-state via PATH (shutil.which).
design: Drop the vendored agent-state from the dotfiles package. Ship agent-monitor-daemon as the only binary. Update installer/config.sh so the registered binary name is agent-monitor-daemon. Keep tmux autostart + @agent_monitor_attention indicator wiring. Depend on steez/bin being on PATH for the daemon to find agent-state.
acceptance_criteria: agent-monitor package contains only agent-monitor-daemon (no vendored agent-state); installer registers binary name agent-monitor-daemon; stow produces ~/.local/bin/agent-monitor-daemon; tmux autostart launches a single daemon after re-source; daemon resolves steez's agent-state via PATH; hidden-window working→attention transition produces exactly one macOS notification and sets the tmux dot; visible-window transition only sets the dot (no notification); daemon shutdown clears all tmux attention options.
notes:
Supersedes dot-ktn.8 (retire legacy) and dot-ktn.9 (audit stale). Those beads are closed as superseded. The dot-ktn.1-.7 work history stays closed — the daemon code and tmux wiring are reused; only the vendoring decision is reversed.
Design spec: plans/dot-y4a-agent-monitor-ship-design-spec.md

title: Audit agent-state changes and revalidate completed agent-monitor beads
description:
Do a cold-start audit of the current `agent-state` implementation and compare it against the assumptions embedded in the completed agent-monitor beads. This audit must decide whether `dot-ktn.2` through `dot-ktn.7` are still good, need patching, or are obsolete.

Audit these concrete files and artifacts:
- `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/plans/sorted-inventing-harp.md`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/agent-monitor/.local/bin/agent-state`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/agent-monitor/.local/bin/agent-monitor-daemon`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/tmux/.config/tmux/tmux.conf`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/installer/config.sh`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/.steez/repo/shared/steez/bin/agent-deliver`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/.steez/repo/shared/steez/bin/agent-watch`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/.steez/repo/shared/steez/bin/agent-watch-daemon`
- `/Users/stevedimakos/.steez/repo/shared/steez/bin/agent-history`

The moving foundation is `agent-state`. Re-check its current CLI behavior, output shape, state names, and any path/runtime assumptions before trusting any completed bead.
design:
Use this audit order:
1. Inspect the current `agent-state` source and behavior first. Compare the vendored copy in dotfiles with the active steez/source version if they diverged.
2. Re-check these assumptions from completed work:
   - `agent-state --all --json` returns a JSON array of pane dicts
   - pane records still use keys the daemon expects, especially `pane`, `agent`, `state`, and `name`
   - state semantics still include `working`, `idle`, and `blocked:*` in a way the daemon logic understands
   - the daemon's tmux enrichment model is still valid
   - the daemon's tmux option name `@agent_monitor_attention` still matches tmux wiring
   - steez-side callers that should use the moved helper now point at `$HOME/.local/bin/agent-state`
3. Re-evaluate each completed bead explicitly:
   - `dot-ktn.2` vendored helper/runtime package
   - `dot-ktn.3` daemon parser, transition logic, singleton/startup behavior
   - `dot-ktn.4` tmux attention syncing and stale cleanup
   - `dot-ktn.5` hidden-window notification trigger logic
   - `dot-ktn.6` tmux autostart and status-line wiring
   - `dot-ktn.7` steez-side consumer migration
4. For each bead, record one verdict only: `still good`, `needs patch`, or `obsolete`, with a concrete reason.
5. If any completed bead needs follow-up work, create new blocking beads before `dot-ktn.8` rather than editing history or hand-waving the gap.
6. Treat `dot-ktn.8` as blocked cleanup until this audit proves parity and compatibility decisions are explicit.
acceptance_criteria: The audit produces a concrete written result covering the files listed in the bead; each completed bead from dot-ktn.2 through dot-ktn.7 is marked `still good`, `needs patch`, or `obsolete` with reasons; any required follow-up beads are created and linked before dot-ktn.8; dot-ktn.8 remains blocked until the audit is finished.
notes: Cold-start context baked in on 2026-04-13: this bead exists because `agent-state` is being actively modified after the thin-slice monitor work landed, so earlier completed beads may now be stale even if they passed at the time.
close_reason: Superseded by dot-y4a. Audit conclusion rolled into the new plan: vendored agent-state drifted structurally (598→1282 lines) but the public contract (--all --json, keys pane/agent/state/name, state vocab idle|working|blocked:*) is still compatible. dot-y4a kills the vendoring entirely and symlinks ~/.local/bin/agent-state into steez, which resolves the drift root cause rather than auditing it repeatedly.

title: Retire legacy steez runtime pieces and the Claude-only fallback after parity
description: After the dotfiles-owned runtime and monitor are proven, remove legacy steez-owned binaries and retire the old Claude-only fallback hook if the new monitor fully covers the use case.
design: This is the cleanup bead. It should happen last, after verification, not as part of the initial ship.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy steez runtime pieces are removed only after consumers move; the Claude-only fallback is removed only if parity is proven; runtime remains reproducible after cleanup.
notes: Not auto-started after dot-ktn.7. Removing the Claude-only fallback is not safe yet because parity is not proven for the daemon-down case, and deleting the steez agent-state original would break ~/.steez/bin/agent-state without an explicit compatibility decision.
close_reason: Superseded by dot-y4a. Retirement of steez-owned runtime was blocked on dot-ktn.9 (audit). The new plan removes the premise of that blocker: by symlinking rather than vendoring, steez remains the owner of agent-state and there is nothing steez-owned to retire. Claude-only Stop hook retirement is deferred until post-ship parity experience; it will get its own bead if/when we pull the trigger.

design: Do the tmux wiring after the monitor works. This bead should not also absorb steez cleanup or hook retirement.
acceptance_criteria: tmux status format shows the monitor-owned indicator; tmux re-source and duplicate startup stay harmless; the monitor starts automatically when the runtime is present.
notes:
Implemented by sub-agent: added guarded tmux autostart for $HOME/.local/bin/agent-monitor-daemon start --detach and wired @agent_monitor_attention into window-status-format only, keeping the current-window format clean.
Locally checked: isolated mini-tmux config showing one start on initial load, no extra starts on re-source, and the indicator format present only in background windows.
Verified by sub-agent: PASS; autostart is guarded/idempotent and the status line shows the monitor-owned indicator.

Verified by sub-agent: PASS; the moved helper path is updated and non-moved helpers remain on steez paths.

title: Add hidden-window macOS notifications while keeping the Claude fallback hook
description: Extend the monitor to send one macOS notification when a hidden agent pane transitions from working to idle or blocked, while leaving the existing Claude-only hook in place during rollout.
design: The goal is parity without removing the safety net. Do not delete or redesign the Claude hook in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Hidden-window working-to-idle and working-to-blocked transitions trigger exactly one notification; visible-window transitions do not; the existing Claude fallback hook still works during migration.
notes:
Implemented by sub-agent: extended agent-monitor-daemon to send one macOS notification for hidden-window working->idle|blocked:* transitions, with visible-window suppression via tmux client window ids, and left the Claude fallback hook untouched.
Locally checked: py_compile plus deterministic hidden-window and visible-window stubbed osascript/tmux harnesses proving 1 vs 0 notifications.
Verified by sub-agent: PASS; hidden transitions notify once, visible ones do not, and the Claude hook was not modified.
close_reason: Added hidden-window macOS notifications without touching the Claude fallback hook.

title: Implement the thin agent monitor core loop against current agent-state output
description: Build one monitor daemon that polls the vendored agent-state output as it exists today, tracks pane and window state, and handles singleton startup cleanly.
design: Do not redesign agent-state JSON here. Consume the current output and keep the loop simple.
acceptance_criteria: One monitor process can be started and re-started safely; it parses current agent-state output without a contract rewrite; it can detect working-to-attention transitions reliably enough for tmux and notification handlers.
notes:
Implemented by sub-agent: added /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/agent-monitor/.local/bin/agent-monitor-daemon with run/start --detach/status, singleton lock + pid under $HOME/.local/state/agent-monitor, current agent-state JSON parsing, tmux metadata enrichment, and working->idle|blocked:* transition emission.
Locally checked: py_compile; deterministic invocation-count stub proving working->idle emission; shell smoke proving start --detach creates a live pidfile/status; duplicate start smoke proving the pid stays the same.
Verified by sub-agent: PASS; singleton startup, current-shape parsing, and transition detection all satisfied.
close_reason: Implemented the thin monitor core loop and verified singleton startup plus transition detection.

title: Add tmux window indicators for background agent attention states
description: Extend the monitor to compute per-window attention state and write a single tmux-owned indicator option for windows containing idle or blocked agent panes.
design: This bead owns tmux option writes and stale-state cleanup, not macOS notifications or autostart wiring.
acceptance_criteria: Background windows show the indicator when attention is needed; windows without monitored attention states are cleared; restart and shutdown do not leave stale indicators behind.
notes:
Implemented by sub-agent: extended agent-monitor-daemon to sync a tmux user option across windows based on idle|blocked panes, clearing stale state on startup/shutdown and when windows stop needing attention.
Locally checked: py_compile and a deterministic stubbed tmux harness proving set/unset behavior plus startup/shutdown cleanup.
Verified by sub-agent: PASS; windows needing attention get the option, others are cleared, and stale state does not linger.
close_reason: Added tmux window attention state syncing with startup and shutdown cleanup.

title: feat: /steez meta-skill — central router + default browse entry point
description:
Create a /steez meta-skill equivalent to /gstack. Central orchestrator that routes user intent to the correct steez-* skill, provides proactive mode control, voice/tone rules, completion protocol, and browse as default action.

## Open Questions (investigate first)
- How does gstack inject routing rules into CLAUDE.md? User-level (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) vs project-level (repo CLAUDE.md) vs configurable? Investigate gstack's implementation before deciding where steez routing lives.

## Section-by-section decisions (from /gstack SKILL.md review)
1. Frontmatter — KEEP (rewrite for steez, drop preamble-tier)
2. Preamble bash — KEEP (use existing steez preamble pattern, strip gstack onboarding flags)
3. Proactive mode behavior — KEEP (5 lines, swap skill name examples)
4. Skill prefix behavior — REMOVE (steez always uses steez-* prefix)
5. Upgrade check handler — REMOVE (steez deploys via stow)
6. Boil the Lake intro — REMOVE (gstack onboarding ceremony)
7. Telemetry opt-in flow — REMOVE (steez logs locally, no opt-in needed)
8. Proactive opt-in flow — REMOVE (config already exists)
9. Routing injection — KEEP routing rules, move to CLAUDE.md (see open question above)
10. Voice — TBD
11-onward — TBD (review paused to file bead and return to gstack removal)

## Depends on
- dot-0fw (tiered preamble system) informs which sections the meta-skill includes

title: Fix bd init failure in ~/.steez/ — empty embeddeddolt
description: bd init --prefix steez in ~/.steez/ reports success but embeddeddolt/steez/ is empty. bd stats then says 'no beads database found'. Likely cause: pre-existing .git or directory structure conflicts with embedded Dolt init. Need to debug why embedded Dolt silently fails. Once fixed, steez-bd should use BEADS_DIR or --db to target ~/.steez/.beads/ for all skill workflow beads.
notes:
Fixed: BEADS_DIR=~/.steez/.beads bypasses git-root discovery. Auto-init on first use for new installs.
Root cause: embedded mode databases are not discoverable by BEADS_DIR or bd where. Fix: re-init with --server mode, which creates a dolt/ directory that bd's discovery recognizes. Auto-init in steez-bd now uses --server flag.
close_reason: Closed

title: steez-smoke — Smoke test all 5 steez skills
description:
After lint passes, smoke test each steez skill to verify it works end-to-end.

## Design Doc
~/.gstack/projects/scratchpad/stevedimakos-unknown-design-20260328-152755.md
See: Success Criteria

## Prerequisites
- All 5 skills ported (dot-527.4 through dot-527.8)
- Lint passed (dot-527.9)
- Helper scripts installed (dot-527.1)
- Config created (dot-527.3)
- Must be run in a real git repo (not scratchpad) — use dumak-netsuite-main or another project

## Smoke tests

### /steez-office-hours
- Invoke in a git repo
- Confirm preamble prints BRANCH, PROACTIVE, REPO_MODE without errors
- Confirm it produces forcing questions (startup or builder mode)
- Confirm end-of-skill suggestion references /steez-plan-ceo-review (not /gstack-*)
- Confirm skill-usage.jsonl entry written to ~/.steez/analytics/

### /steez-plan-ceo-review
- Invoke after office-hours produces a design doc
- Confirm it finds the design doc in ~/.steez/projects/
- Confirm it enters plan mode
- Confirm end-of-skill suggestion references /steez-plan-eng-review
- Confirm review log written to ~/.steez/projects/$SLUG/

### /steez-plan-eng-review
- Invoke after ceo-review
- Confirm it finds the design doc
- Confirm review sections run (architecture, code quality, tests, performance)
- Confirm review log written
- Confirm Review Readiness Dashboard renders

### /steez-review
- Invoke on a feature branch with changes
- Confirm it analyzes the diff
- Confirm review log written

### /steez-ship
- Invoke on a feature branch
- Confirm it detects base branch
- Confirm Review Readiness Dashboard renders
- Confirm it proceeds toward PR creation (can abort before actual push)

## Cross-cutting checks
- [ ] No "gstack" references in any preamble output
- [ ] skill-usage.jsonl accumulates entries in ~/.steez/analytics/
- [ ] Skill Self-Report section present (verify by triggering a minor issue)
- [ ] Helper failure warnings work: temporarily rename steez-config, invoke a skill, confirm warning appears
- [ ] Voice identity shows "senior engineering partner" framing, not "GStack" or "Garry Tan"

## Known limitations
- /steez-review and /steez-ship require a real git repo with branches and changes
- Browse-dependent features (visual sketch in office-hours) depend on steez browse binary being built
- Codex-dependent features (outside voice) require codex CLI + a git repo
close_reason: Structural smoke test passed: all 5 SKILL.md files exist with correct frontmatter, 5 bin scripts executable, runtime dirs created, config readable, steez-slug works, preamble runs clean (fixed || && precedence bug in all 5 skills). Interactive skill invocation deferred to first real use.

## Path replacements (apply globally)

| /gstack-office-hours | /steez-office-hours |

| /gstack-plan-eng-review | /steez-plan-eng-review |

| ~/.claude/skills/gstack/ETHOS.md | ~/.claude/skills/steez/ETHOS.md |
| ~/.claude/skills/gstack/office-hours/SKILL.md | ~/.claude/skills/steez-office-hours/SKILL.md |
| ~/.claude/skills/gstack/browse/dist/gstack-browse | ~/.claude/skills/steez/browse/dist/browse |

## Browse SETUP block
Replace with repo-local + global fallback for steez browse:
```bash
_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)
B=""
[ -n "$_ROOT" ] && [ -x "$_ROOT/.claude/skills/steez/browse/dist/browse" ] && B="$_ROOT/.claude/skills/steez/browse/dist/browse"

if [ -x "$B" ]; then echo "READY: $B"; else echo "NEEDS_SETUP"; fi
```

## Fallback warnings
Replace silent `2>/dev/null || true` on helper calls with visible warnings:
```bash
"$STEEZ_BIN/steez-review-log" '...' 2>/dev/null || echo "[steez] WARNING: review-log failed" >&2
```

- All skill chaining refs point to /steez-*

title: steez-fork — Fork 5 gstack workflow skills into steez namespace
description:
Fork 5 core gstack workflow skills (office-hours, plan-ceo-review, plan-eng-review, review, ship) into a steez-* namespace in dotfiles. Full independence from gstack for these skills.

## Design Doc
~/.gstack/projects/scratchpad/stevedimakos-unknown-design-20260328-152755.md

## Goals
- Own the workflow skills (no upstream changes affect them)
- Strip dead code (~107 lines/skill): onboarding conditionals, contributor mode (repurposed), remote telemetry, version checks, skill_prefix logic
- Tailor voice (new identity line, remove Garry Tan/YC branding)
- Decouple data directory (~/.steez/ instead of ~/.gstack/)
- Rename all helper scripts from gstack-* to steez-*

## Architecture
- Skills: dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez-{name}/SKILL.md
- Bin scripts: dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez/bin/steez-{name}
- Runtime state: ~/.steez/ (config, sessions, analytics, projects, skill-reports)
- Browse binary: dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez/browse/dist/browse (existing, WIP)
- STEEZ_BIN="$HOME/.claude/skills/steez/bin"
- STEEZ_HOME="$HOME/.steez"

## Key Decisions
- steez-* prefix for all skills and scripts (no naming conflicts with gstack)
- 5 helper scripts (dropped gstack-repo-mode, REPO_MODE=solo hardcoded)
- Visible warnings on helper failures (not silent no-ops)
- Contributor Mode repurposed as "Skill Self-Report" (always on, writes to ~/.steez/skill-reports/)
- Voice identity: "You are a senior engineering partner — a CTO-level operator who ships product and owns it in production."
- Browse SETUP block: repo-local first, global fallback
- Provenance tracked in FORK_MANIFEST.md
- Config: only proactive: true needed
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Implement ns cancel + ns-cancel.test.ts
description: State recovery command. Clear current sublist line (if editing), return to form view. Handles: mid-line-edit cancel, dialog dismissal during cancel. Return { cancelled: boolean, previousState, currentState }. Integration test: src/ns/test/ns-cancel.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ns status + ns-status.test.ts
description: NS-specific page state: record type (from URL pattern), mode (create/edit/view), session validity (check for login redirect patterns). URL pattern detection for record types. Return { url, recordType, recordId, mode, sessionValid, environment }. Integration test: src/ns/test/ns-status.test.ts with various URL patterns.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ns query (SuiteQL via fetch) + ns-query.test.ts
description: Execute SuiteQL via fetch('/services/rest/query/v1/suiteql', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Prefer': 'transient' }, body: JSON.stringify({ q: sql }) }) inside page.evaluate(). SELECT-only validation: reject anything not matching SELECT or WITH...SELECT patterns. Explicitly block INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, CREATE, ALTER keywords (defense in depth). Uses browser session cookies for auth. Return { results, count, hasMore, elapsed_ms }. Unit test for validation + integration test: src/ns/test/ns-query.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ns save with concurrency + DOM modal detection + ns-save.test.ts
description: Click Save, wait for result (30s timeout). Detect BOTH native browser dialogs (page.on('dialog')) AND NetSuite DOM modals (N/ui/dialog containers, .uir-message-error, #_err_alert) via DOM inspection after save. Concurrency detection: check for 'record has been changed' pattern → typed ConcurrencyError. Also detect: client saveRecord cancellation, server postback errors, workflow redirects, lock conflicts. Idempotency guard: check URL for ?id= before retry. Port validation.ts's 4 error surfaces + add N/ui/dialog detection. Integration test: src/ns/test/ns-save.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ns add-row with sublist convergence + ns-add-row.test.ts
description: Add sublist row with field values, commit. Unified entity-ref detection for sublist cells (same _display detection as ns set). Convergence polling extended to sublist cells: after setting entity-ref column (e.g. item), poll other columns (rate, amount, taxcode) via nlapiGetCurrentLineItemValue until stable. Same algorithm as body fields. Idempotency guard: check line count before retry. Dialog text capture. Return { sublist, lineNumber, values, settled, elapsed_ms, dialog }. Integration test: src/ns/test/ns-add-row.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ns set with auto-detect cascading + ns-set.test.ts
description: Auto-detect field type via _display companion element. Entity-ref fields: fire cascading (nlapiSetFieldValue(id, val, false, false)), wait via convergence polling, snapshot before/after, return diff. Other fields: suppress cascading (nlapiSetFieldValue(id, val, true, true)), return immediately. --source flag forces fire, --no-source forces suppress. Dialog capture on set. Return { fieldId, value, settled, elapsed_ms, diff: { changed, newMandatory }, dialog }. Integration test: src/ns/test/ns-set.test.ts with nlapi stubs simulating sourcing cascades.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ns inspect with dynamic sublist discovery + ns-inspect.test.ts
description: Form graph via NS client APIs. All field IDs, labels, types, mandatory flags, current values, enabled/disabled state. Entity-ref detection via _display companion. Dropdown options via getSelectOptions(). Dynamic sublist discovery from form DOM (NOT hardcoded) — this is partially greenfield (sublist row extraction has no existing driver code). Sublist columns introspected from header row DOM. Sublist line values on existing records (edit/view mode). @ref integration for element selection (moved from Phase 3). Form mode detection: create/edit/view. Return structured JSON blob. Integration test: src/ns/test/ns-inspect.test.ts with mock NS page fixture.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement ns navigate + ns-navigate.test.ts
description: Port from existing driver navigation.ts. Record type URL map from Tier 1 constants. Navigate to new record (ns navigate salesorder) or existing (ns navigate salesorder --id 12345). Session-aware guard: check NS API availability + session expiry before navigating. Detect record type from page after navigation. Return { url, recordType, mode, sessionValid }. Integration test: src/ns/test/ns-navigate.test.ts with HTML fixture stubbing nlapi* globals.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement command mutex (per-page request queue) + mutex.test.ts
description: Per-page request queue in the daemon. NS commands with convergence polling can take 5+ seconds — concurrent commands must queue, not race. One command executes at a time per page. FIFO ordering. All page.evaluate calls in NS commands have explicit timeouts (10s for inspect, 5s for convergence, 30s for save). On timeout, return partial results + error — never hang. Unit test: src/ns/test/mutex.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement typed error taxonomy + recovery model + errors.test.ts
description: Types: ValidationError | ConcurrencyError | SessionExpired | SaveTimeout | NotARecordPage. Each error includes { type, recoverable: boolean, suggestedAction: string }. NS API availability guard: check typeof nlapiGetField !== 'undefined' before executing. Session expiry detection via URL pattern. Every NS command returns structured result with timing, error type, before/after snapshot diff on write commands, dialog text if fired. Unit test: src/ns/test/errors.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Tier 1 universal NS knowledge constants + tier1.test.ts
description: Static TypeScript constants for ~5-8 admission-controlled universal NS platform facts. Admitted: (1) entity-ref sourcing cascades, (2) rate not always sourced from pricing, (3) PO line location independent of header, (4) informational vs blocking dialog patterns, (5) standard sublists per record type, (6) record type URL map. Format: exported const objects, not JSON/markdown. Unit test: src/ns/test/tier1.test.ts validates constants structure and completeness.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement convergence polling algorithm + convergence.test.ts
description: Core settle-detection: after setting a field, snapshot changed fields (diff-only, not full-form) and poll until values stabilize across 3 consecutive stable polls (600ms gap tolerance). Uses batch page.evaluate (single call returns {[fieldId]: value} map, O(1) roundtrips). Polling: 50ms start, backoff to 200ms, max 5s timeout. title: Distribute workspaces across monitors like AeroSpace
description:
## Problem
Rift gives each display its own independent 10 workspaces (1-0). User wants the AeroSpace model: 10 global workspaces distributed across monitors.

- Main monitor (VG279QE5A (1), screen_id=3): workspaces 1, 2, 3, 4
- Left monitor (VG279QE5A (2), screen_id=2): workspaces 5, 6, 7
- MacBook (Built-in Retina, screen_id=1): workspaces 8, 9, 0

## Current Behavior
Each monitor bar shows all 10 workspaces (1-0) because Rift has 10 per display. Pressing opt+5 switches workspace 5 on the CURRENT display — it doesn't jump to the left monitor.

## Desired Behavior
Workspace numbers are globally unique. opt+5 focuses the left monitor and activates its first workspace. opt+shift+5 moves focused window there.

## What Needs To Change

### SketchyBar (spaces_rift.lua)
Add a workspace assignment map (which global workspace numbers go to which display). Each bar only shows its assigned subset with the correct global labels. For example, the left monitor bar shows items labeled 5, 6, 7 that map to Rift workspace indices 0, 1, 2 on that display's space.

### Rift Keybindings
Each global workspace hotkey (opt+N) needs to become a compound command:
1. Focus the correct display: rift-cli execute display focus --index <display_index>
2. Switch to the local workspace: rift-cli execute workspace switch <local_index>

Similarly opt+shift+N (move window) would use rift-cli execute display move-window + workspace switch.

### Rift CLI Reference
- rift-cli execute display focus --index <0-based>
- rift-cli execute display move-window --index <0-based>
- rift-cli execute workspace switch <0-based>
- rift-cli execute workspace move-window <0-based>

## Open Questions
- Where should the workspace-to-display assignment map live? (settings.lua? rift config? both?)
- Does the Rift config already have keybindings that need updating, or is that separate?
- Should the map be hardcoded per monitor profile (like the old AeroSpace settings.monitors.profiles) or dynamic?
close_reason: Stale — AeroSpace replaced by Rift, workspace model changed

title: Replace aerospace laptop-mode comment-sniff with file-identity check
description:
items/spaces.lua:17-25 is_laptop_profile_active() decides whether to render 5 vs 10 workspaces by reading ~/.config/aerospace/aerospace.toml and grepping for the literal comment 'Laptop-only setup'. Any comment edit or TOML reserialization silently breaks laptop-mode detection when externals are still physically connected.

Context: aerospace profile switching means copying one of aerospace-{home,laptop,office}.toml onto aerospace.toml (per the current dotfiles layout). The comment only happens to live in the laptop version — fragile coupling.

Do NOT delete is_laptop_profile_active(): the case 'laptop profile active while externals still physically connected' is real and distinct from is_laptop_only. The fix is to replace the signal, not remove it.
design:
Pure-Lua file compare against aerospace-laptop.toml — no shell, no path-quoting concerns, no subprocess cost:

  local function files_identical(a, b)
    local fa = io.open(a, "rb"); if not fa then return false end
    local fb = io.open(b, "rb"); if not fb then fa:close(); return false end
    local identical = true
    while true do
      local ca = fa:read(8192)
      local cb = fb:read(8192)
      if ca ~= cb then identical = false; break end
      if ca == nil then break end
    end
    fa:close(); fb:close()
    return identical
  end

  local function is_laptop_profile_active()
    local home = os.getenv("HOME")
    if not home then return false end
    return files_identical(
      home .. "/.config/aerospace/aerospace.toml",
      home .. "/.config/aerospace/aerospace-laptop.toml"
    )
  end

Robust to comment edits, reformatting, anything — as long as profile switching keeps meaning 'copy one profile file onto aerospace.toml'.
acceptance_criteria:
1. cp aerospace-laptop.toml aerospace.toml; reload sketchybar with externals attached → 5 workspaces render.
2. cp aerospace-home.toml aerospace.toml; reload → 10 workspaces render.
3. Add a comment to aerospace-laptop.toml and also to the active aerospace.toml → detection still returns true (file contents still match).
4. Change one byte in aerospace.toml without re-copying the profile → returns false (expected — aerospace.toml no longer matches the laptop profile).
close_reason: Replaced comment-sniff with pure-Lua chunked byte compare (files_identical). Verified against all three aerospace profiles: active == home → true, active == laptop → false, active == office → false, missing path → false. luac clean, sketchybar --reload exit 0.

title: Fix spaces.lua nil-guards on sbar.exec callbacks
description:
Two sbar.exec callbacks in items/spaces.lua dereference possibly-nil output. If the aerospace CLI is missing, fails, or returns nothing, the callback crashes and the subscription handler dies silently.

Sites:
- items/spaces.lua:108-110 — monitor_output:gmatch and :find with no nil check. Crash here aborts the entire spaces init chain; the UI never renders.
- items/spaces.lua:258-262 — f:gsub in the aerospace_workspace_change fallback branch (env.FOCUSED_WORKSPACE empty). Crash here kills the subscription so workspace switching stops updating.

These are inconsistencies with the file's own convention: three other sbar.exec callbacks (if ws_list, if apps, if app_name) already nil-check.
design:
Line 108-110: add at top of callback
  if not monitor_output or monitor_output == "" then return end
before any string method call.

Line 258-262: replace f:gsub with
  local clean_f = f and f:gsub("%s+", "") or ""
  if clean_f == "" then return end
before update_highlight(clean_f).

Scope limit: guards prevent the crash and leave the subscription alive. They do NOT add retry/re-init logic. If sketchybar starts while aerospace is down, spaces init still returns early and the UI stays empty until a manual reload. Actual recovery when aerospace comes back would require re-running fetch_monitor_workspaces — out of scope for this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
1. kill aerospace daemon; reload sketchybar — spaces UI does NOT crash the subscription; it degrades to empty/default and does not propagate Lua errors to the event loop.
2. start sketchybar while aerospace is running; kill aerospace mid-session; switch workspaces via any aerospace client — handler does not die, other items (calendar, battery) keep updating.
close_reason: Added nil-guards at items/spaces.lua:110 (monitor_output early return) and :260 (f:gsub guarded with default empty, early return on empty). luac -p passes, sketchybar --reload exit 0. Per design, guards prevent the crash; they do NOT add recovery logic if init races with aerospace being down. That was explicitly scoped out.

title: Claude Code /buddy companion patch
description: Re-apply custom companion after Claude Code updates. Salt: friend-04485077 → legendary shiny ghost (DEBUG 86, PAT 89, CHAOS 50, WIS 100, SNARK 40). Three same-length binary replacements + ad-hoc codesign. See body for full steps.
notes:
## Buddy Patch Steps

Run if /buddy shows wrong stats or COMMON rarity after a Claude Code update.

### 1. Patch the binary
```bash
python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess, glob

# Find current binary
versions = glob.glob(str(Path.home() / '.local/share/claude/versions/*'))
binary = max((v for v in versions if not v.endswith(('.prev', '.prev2', '.prev3', '.backup', '.original'))), key=lambda x: x)

p = Path(binary)
b = p.read_bytes()

# Check if already patched
if b'friend-04485077' in b:
    print('Already patched.')
    exit(0)

# Three same-length replacements
b = b.replace(b'friend-2026-401', b'friend-04485077')
b = b.replace(b'shiny:H()<0.01', b'shiny:H()<1.00')
b = b.replace(
    b'LN6={common:60,uncommon:25,rare:10,epic:4,legendary:1}',
    b'LN6={common:00,uncommon:00,rare:00,epic:0,legendary:9}',
)
p.write_bytes(b)
print(f'Patched {binary}')
"

# Re-sign
codesign -f -s - ~/.local/share/claude/versions/*[!.]*
```

### 2. Clear companion config
```bash
python3 -c "
import json
with open('$HOME/.claude.json', 'r') as f:
    d = json.load(f)
d.pop('companion', None)
d.pop('companionMuted', None)
with open('$HOME/.claude.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(d, f, indent=2)
    f.write('\n')
"
```

### 3. Restart Claude Code and run /buddy

### Expected result
- LEGENDARY SHINY GHOST
- DEBUG ~86, PAT ~89, CHAOS ~50, WIS ~100, SNARK ~40

### 4. Set optimized personality (after hatch)
```bash
python3 -c "
import json
with open('$HOME/.claude.json', 'r') as f:
    d = json.load(f)
d['companion']['personality'] = 'A legendary ghost with perfect recall of your codebase, who spots bugs the instant they appear and explains the fix like a patient mentor — never rushed, always showing you exactly where to look.'
d['companion']['name'] = 'Flintwick'
with open('$HOME/.claude.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(d, f, indent=2)
    f.write('\n')
"
```

### Personality design notes
- 195 chars (API cap is 200, truncates silently)
- Optimized for stat spread: WIS 100, PAT 89, DEBUG 86, CHAOS 50, SNARK 40
- No neg flavor (no snark, no temporal confusion, no judgment)
- "patient mentor" archetype gives model a positive voice without quirk tax
- Previous personality was 257 chars (57 over cap, truncated mid-sentence)

### To change name only (keeps stats/species)
```bash
python3 -c "
import json
with open('$HOME/.claude.json', 'r') as f:
    d = json.load(f)
d['companion']['name'] = 'YOUR_NAME_HERE'
with open('$HOME/.claude.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(d, f, indent=2)
    f.write('\n')
"
```

### How it works
- Salt seeds Bun.hash(accountUuid + salt) → wyhash PRNG
- PRNG order: rarity → species → eye → hat → shiny → stats
- Salt change rerolls everything; name/personality are config-only
- friend-04485077 was found by brute-forcing 5M salts filtered for ghost + low snark + high wisdom
- Personality generated by LLM (temperature=1) at hatch, then stored in ~/.claude.json
- buddy_react API sends personality.slice(0,200) — hard cap, no warning
- April Fools 2026 feature, may be removed in future versions
close_reason: Personality optimized: 257 chars (truncated) → 195 chars (under 200 cap). Removed neg flavor, kept Flintwick name (collision-proof). Bead notes updated with full patch steps + personality design notes for coworker sharing.

title: Go installer: Bubble Tea TUI wizard
description:
## What
Port the steez Bubble Tea TUI to the dotfiles installer. 6-step wizard: splash → mode → picker → preflight → install → summary.

## Depends On
- Bead 1 (scaffold): `internal/platform` package (read `internal/platform/detect.go`)
- Bead 2 (manifest): `internal/manifest` package (read `internal/manifest/manifest.go`)

## Files to Create
- `internal/tui/theme.go` — Lipgloss styles (port directly from steez)
- `internal/tui/picker.go` — category multi-select (port from steez, adapt for 7 categories + OS filtering)
- `internal/tui/setup.go` — 6-step Bubble Tea wizard model

## Port From Steez (read these files)
- `~/Projects/Personal/steez/internal/tui/theme.go` — copy directly, same ANSI colors
- `~/Projects/Personal/steez/internal/tui/picker.go` — adapt: 7 categories not 5, filter packages by platform via pkgmgr.Resolve(), add disabled state for uninstallable packages
- `~/Projects/Personal/steez/internal/tui/setup.go` — adapt: different steps, async install with progress

## Theme (internal/tui/theme.go)
Copy steez theme.go exactly. 14 Lipgloss styles. ANSI colors (not hex). Respects NO_COLOR env var.

## Picker (internal/tui/picker.go)
```go
type pickerItem struct {
    isCategory   bool
    categoryName string
    pkgName      string
    description  string
    selected     bool
    disabled     bool  // uninstallable on this platform
}

type pickerModel struct {
    items        []pickerItem
    cursor       int
    scrollOffset int
    width, height int
    styles       Styles
    done, quitting bool
}

func newPicker(m *manifest.Manifest, plat platform.Info, styles Styles, width int) pickerModel
func (p pickerModel) SelectedPackages() []string
```

Category order: `["macos", "shell", "dev", "ai", "file", "git", "system"]`
- Skip categories where OSFilter excludes current platform (e.g. "macos" hidden on Linux)
- Within each category, disable packages that have no install method for current platform
- Keybindings: j/k or arrows (move), space (toggle), a (toggle all), enter (confirm if count > 0), q/esc (quit)
- Viewport scrolling with scroll indicators (same as steez)

## Setup Model (internal/tui/setup.go)
```go
type step int
const (
    stepSplash    step = iota
    stepMode
    stepPicker
    stepPreflight
    stepInstall
    stepSummary
)

type installResult struct {
    name      string
    binaryOK  bool
    binaryMsg string
    configOK  bool
    configMsg string
}

type setupModel struct {
    step          step
    width, height int
    styles        Styles
    repoPath      string
    manifest      *manifest.Manifest
    platform      platform.Info

    // Step state
    modeCursor    int      // 0=full, 1=custom
    modeChoice    string
    picker        pickerModel
    selectedPkgs  []string
    results       []installResult
    installIdx    int
    installDone   bool
    quitting      bool
    err           error

    // Injected install function (set by caller in wire-up bead)
    installFunc   func(pkgName string) installResult
}
```

### Step Details
- **Splash:** Show "dotfiles v3.0.0" + system info (OS, arch, distro, home). 1 second auto-advance or any key.
- **Mode:** Two options: "Full Setup" (auto-select all platform-compatible packages) and "Custom Selection" (open picker). Cursor nav.
- **Picker:** Category multi-select (see above). Only shown for Custom mode.
- **Preflight:** Show count of selected packages. Confirm with enter or q to go back.
- **Install:** Async. Each package fires a `tea.Cmd` that calls `installFunc` and returns an `installMsg`. View shows growing results list with spinner on current package. Format: `[03/12] ◆ nvim  ✔ Binary INSTALLED  ✔ Config LINKED`
- **Summary:** Show stats (X installed, X existed, X failed). List any failures. Show "Next steps: restart your terminal."

### Async Install Pattern
```go
type installMsg struct {
    result installResult
}

func (m setupModel) runNextInstall() tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        result := m.installFunc(m.selectedPkgs[m.installIdx])
        return installMsg{result: result}
    }
}
```
Install one package at a time (sequential, not parallel — avoids package manager conflicts).

## Verification
- `go build ./cmd/dotfiles` compiles
- TUI launches without crash
- Picker shows categories with correct packages for platform
- Picker scrolls, selects, confirms
- Install step shows progress (can use a mock installFunc for testing)

## Context
Bead 6 of 7. The `installFunc` field on `setupModel` is injected by the wire-up bead (bead 7) — it connects the TUI to the actual pkgmgr/stow/hooks engine. For standalone TUI testing, use a mock that returns fake results with a sleep.

title: Go installer: hooks (shell-out stubs + Omarchy ghostty merge)
description:
## What
Build the post-install hook system. Most hooks shell out to existing bash functions. The Omarchy ghostty merge is implemented in pure Go.

## Depends On
- Bead 1 (scaffold): `internal/platform` package (read `internal/platform/detect.go` for types)

## Files to Create
- `internal/hooks/hooks.go` — hook registry, runner, phase definitions
- `internal/hooks/zsh.go` — zsh setup hook
- `internal/hooks/git.go` — git config hook
- `internal/hooks/fonts.go` — nerd font install hook
- `internal/hooks/theme.go` — theme selection hook (macOS only)
- `internal/hooks/tmux.go` — TPM + tmux-which-key hook
- `internal/hooks/raycast.go` — extension build hook
- `internal/hooks/opencode.go` — plugin install hook
- `internal/hooks/omarchy.go` — Omarchy ghostty config merge (pure Go)
- `internal/hooks/hooks_test.go`

## Core Types
```go
type Phase int
const (
    PreInstall  Phase = iota // runs before any packages install (e.g. zsh zdotdir bootstrap)
    PostBinary               // runs after binary install, before stow (e.g. per-package hooks)
    PostStow                 // runs after stow completes (e.g. plugin installs, tmux TPM)
)

type HookContext struct {
    Platform   platform.Info
    RepoDir    string  // dotfiles repo root
    Home       string
    PkgName    string
    AutoAccept bool    // --yes mode, skip interactive prompts
}

type HookFunc func(ctx HookContext) error

func Register(name string, phase Phase, fn HookFunc)
func RunPhase(phase Phase, ctx HookContext) error
func RunHook(name string, ctx HookContext) error
```

## Shell-Out Pattern
Most hooks call existing bash functions. They MUST source the full installer runtime because functions depend on shared globals ($DOTFILES_DIR, $OS, $DISTRO, $IS_OMARCHY, theme vars) and helper functions (log_success, ui_confirm, gum).

Shell-out template:
```go
func shellHook(ctx HookContext, functionName string, extraSources ...string) error {
    script := fmt.Sprintf(
        "export DOTFILES_DIR=%q OS=%q DISTRO=%q IS_OMARCHY=%q HOME=%q; "+
        "source %q/installer/bootstrap.sh; "+
        "source %q/installer/config.sh; "+
        "source %q/installer/logging.sh; "+
        "source %q/installer/ui.sh; "+
        "%s; %s",
        ctx.RepoDir, ctx.Platform.OS, ctx.Platform.Distro,
        boolStr(ctx.Platform.IsOmarchy), ctx.Home,
        ctx.RepoDir, ctx.RepoDir, ctx.RepoDir, ctx.RepoDir,
        strings.Join(extraSources, "; "),
        functionName,
    )
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "-c", script)
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin // for interactive prompts
    return cmd.Run()
}
```

## Hook Registry
```go
func init() {
    Register("zsh_setup", PreInstall, runZshSetup)           // source zsh_setup.sh, call setup_zsh_env
    Register("git_config", PostStow, runGitConfig)            // source git_setup.sh, call setup_git_config
    Register("nerd_fonts", PostStow, runNerdFonts)            // source install.sh, call install_nerd_fonts
    Register("tmux_plugins", PostStow, runTmuxPlugins)        // source install.sh, call setup_tmux_plugins
    Register("raycast_extensions", PostStow, runRaycastExts)  // source install.sh, call setup_raycast_core_extensions
    Register("opencode_plugins", PostStow, runOpenCodePlugins)// source install.sh, call setup_opencode_plugins
    Register("theme_select", PostStow, runThemeSelect)        // source install.sh, call theme selection flow
    Register("omarchy_ghostty", PostBinary, runOmarchyGhostty) // pure Go, no shell-out
}
```

## Omarchy Ghostty Merge (pure Go, internal/hooks/omarchy.go)
When `IsOmarchy` is true and package is "ghostty":
1. Read `repoDir/ghostty/.config/ghostty/config`
2. Extract personal preferences: lines matching `font-size`, `keybind`, `split-*`, `unfocused-split-*`, `clipboard-*`, `background-opacity`
3. Skip: `theme`, `font-family` (Omarchy manages these)
4. Write extracted prefs to `~/.config/ghostty/steez.conf`
5. Read Omarchy config at `~/.config/ghostty/config`
6. If `config-file = ~/.config/ghostty/steez.conf` not present, append it
7. Warn on keybind conflicts (same keybind different action)

Reference for Omarchy ghostty logic: `~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/installer/install.sh` function `setup_ghostty_steez_config()`

## Which Hooks Run When
The caller (bead 7, wire-up) decides when to invoke hooks based on which packages were selected:
- If "zsh" selected → run zsh_setup (PreInstall)
- If "ghostty" on Omarchy → run omarchy_ghostty (PostBinary) instead of stow
- If "opencode" selected → run opencode_plugins (PostStow)
- If "raycast" selected → run raycast_extensions (PostStow)
- If "tmux" selected → run tmux_plugins (PostStow)
- If macOS and theme-able packages selected → run theme_select (PostStow)
- Always run git_config and nerd_fonts (PostStow) if those packages selected

## Verification
- `go test ./internal/hooks/...` passes
- Test: registry returns error for unknown hook name
- Test: RunPhase executes all hooks for given phase
- Test: Omarchy ghostty merge creates steez.conf with correct prefs
- Test: Omarchy ghostty merge adds config-file include line
- Test: Omarchy ghostty merge skips theme/font-family lines

## Context
Bead 5 of 7. The shell-out approach is a pragmatic bridge — hooks call existing bash functions that already work. Later beads will rewrite hooks in pure Go. The Omarchy ghostty merge is the one hook implemented in pure Go from the start because it is simpler than sourcing the bash runtime for that specific case.
notes: PATH CHANGE: internal/hooks/ → installer/internal/hooks/. Bash scripts that hooks source are now siblings in installer/ (same directory, not parent). Shell-out paths change from ctx.RepoDir/installer/bootstrap.sh to just the installer dir since Go code is already inside installer/.

title: Go installer: stow wrapper with conflict detection
description:
## What
Build the GNU stow wrapper that handles config symlink management: conflict detection, backup, no-folding, and Omarchy routing.

## Depends On
- Bead 1 (scaffold): `internal/platform` package with `Info` struct (read `internal/platform/detect.go` for types)

## Files to Create
- `internal/stow/stow.go` + `stow_test.go`

## Types and Functions
```go
type Result int
const (
    Linked  Result = iota // newly linked
    Already               // already stowed
    NoConfig              // no stow_dir for this package
    Failed                // error
)

type Options struct {
    Dir       string // --dir (dotfiles repo root)
    Target    string // --target ($HOME)
    NoFolding bool   // --no-folding
    Restow   bool   // --restow (default true)
    Force     bool   // auto-accept conflicts (headless --yes mode)
    Platform  platform.Info
}

func Run(pkg string, opts Options) (Result, error)
func CheckConflicts(pkg string, opts Options) ([]string, error)
func IsStowed(pkg string, opts Options) bool
func BackupConflicts(pkg string, conflicts []string, repoDir string) error
```

## Implementation Details

### Run()
1. Check if stow dir exists at `opts.Dir/pkg` — if not, return NoConfig
2. Check `IsStowed()` — if true, return Already
3. Omarchy routing: if `opts.Platform.IsOmarchy` and pkg is "nvim", return NoConfig (skip, Omarchy owns it)
4. Check conflicts via `CheckConflicts()`
5. If conflicts and `opts.Force`: auto-backup via `BackupConflicts()`
6. If conflicts and not Force: return error with conflict list
7. Execute: `stow --dir=DIR --target=TARGET --restow [--no-folding] PKG`
8. Return Linked

### CheckConflicts()
- Run `stow --no --verbose=2 --dir=DIR --target=TARGET --restow [--no-folding] PKG`
- Parse stderr for conflict patterns (must handle BOTH stow versions):
  - stow 2.3.x: "existing target is not a symlink: PATH"
  - stow 2.3.x: "existing target is neither a link nor a directory: PATH"
  - stow 2.4+: "cannot stow ... over existing target PATH since ..."
- Return list of conflicting paths

### IsStowed()
- Run `stow --no --verbose --dir=DIR --target=TARGET --restow PKG`
- If no LINK operations in output → already stowed
- Do NOT use `--restow` for this check (always shows UNLINK/LINK)

### BackupConflicts()
- Create backup dir: `repoDir/.backups/TIMESTAMP/PKG/`
- Move each conflicting file to backup dir, preserving relative path
- Return error if any move fails

## Testing (integration tests with t.TempDir)
Use `t.TempDir()` as fake $HOME. Create a fake dotfiles repo structure in another temp dir.
- Test clean stow creates expected symlinks
- Test re-stow is idempotent
- Test conflict detection catches real files
- Test backup moves files to .backups/
- Test --no-folding creates file symlinks not directory symlinks
- Test Omarchy nvim skip
- Note: these tests require `stow` to be installed on the test machine

## Reference Code
- Bash stow logic: `~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/installer/install.sh` (functions `stow_package()`, `check_stow_conflicts()`, `is_stowed()`, `backup_conflicts()`)
- Steez symlink patterns (for inspiration, different approach): `~/Projects/Personal/steez/internal/installer/symlink.go`

## Context
Bead 4 of 7. The stow wrapper shells out to the `stow` CLI via exec.Command. Stow is a runtime dependency — the Go installer should install stow itself first (it is in DEV_PKGS) before running any stow operations.
notes: PATH CHANGE: internal/stow/ → installer/internal/stow/. Module path: github.com/st-eez/dotfiles-cli.

title: Go installer: package manager abstraction layer
description:
## What
Build the package manager abstraction that resolves which tool to use (brew/pacman/apt/npm/corepack/native) for each package on the current platform, and executes the install.

## Depends On
- Bead 1 (scaffold): `internal/platform` package with `Info` struct
- Bead 2 (manifest): `internal/manifest` package with `Package`, `InstallMethod` types

Read those files first to understand the types you consume.

## Files to Create
- `internal/pkgmgr/manager.go` — interface + Resolve() + IsInstalled()
- `internal/pkgmgr/brew.go` — Homebrew manager
- `internal/pkgmgr/pacman.go` — pacman + AUR (yay/paru) manager
- `internal/pkgmgr/apt.go` — apt-get manager
- `internal/pkgmgr/alt.go` — npm, corepack, native function runners
- `internal/pkgmgr/manager_test.go`

## Interface
```go
type Manager interface {
    Install(target string) error
    Name() string
}

func Resolve(pkg manifest.Package, plat platform.Info) (Manager, string, error)
func IsInstalled(binaryName string) bool
```

## Manager Implementations

### BrewManager
- `brew install <target>` — handles `--cask` prefix (e.g. "--cask ghostty" → `brew install --cask ghostty`)
- Wraps via `exec.Command("brew", "install", args...)`

### PacmanManager
- `sudo pacman -S --noconfirm --needed <target>`
- For AUR packages (prefixed "aur:" in manifest): detect yay or paru, use that instead

### AptManager
- `sudo apt-get install -y <target>`

### NpmManager
- `npm install -g <target>`

### CorepackManager
- `corepack enable <target>`, fallback to npm if corepack unavailable

### NativeRunner
- Maps Go function names (from manifest `Native` field) to Go functions
- Registry pattern: `var nativeFuncs = map[string]func(platform.Info) error{}`
- Functions are stubs for now (log "would run X"), actual implementations come in later beads

## Resolve() Logic
1. Get install method: `manifest.ResolveInstallMethod(pkg, plat.Distro)`
2. Check which field is set (exactly one should be non-empty)
3. Return the matching Manager + target string
4. Error if no method found

## IsInstalled() Logic
- `exec.LookPath(binaryName)` for CLI tools
- For macOS apps: check `/Applications/<Name>.app` and `~/Applications/<Name>.app`

## Verification
- `go test ./internal/pkgmgr/...` passes
- Test: Resolve returns BrewManager for macOS brew package
- Test: Resolve returns PacmanManager for Arch pacman package
- Test: Resolve returns NpmManager for "all" npm package
- Test: Resolve returns NativeRunner for native function
- Test: Resolve errors for package with no method on platform
- Test: IsInstalled finds `git` (always present)

## Reference Code
- Bash install logic: `~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/installer/install.sh` (function `install_package()`)
- Bash config mappings: `~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/installer/config.sh`

## Context
Bead 3 of 7. Reads types from `internal/platform/detect.go` and `internal/manifest/manifest.go`.

title: Go installer: package manifest (packages.json + parser)
description:
## What
Create the data-driven package manifest for the dotfiles installer. This replaces the bash functions `get_brew_pkg()`, `get_pacman_pkg()`, `get_apt_pkg()`, `get_binary_name()`, `get_alt_install_method()`, and `get_pkg_description()` from `installer/config.sh`.

## Files to Create
- `packages.json` — all 47 packages with cross-platform install methods
- `internal/manifest/manifest.go` + `manifest_test.go` — JSON parser, validator, resolution

## Types (internal/manifest/manifest.go)
```go
type Manifest struct {
    Version    string               `json:"version"`
    Categories map[string]Category  `json:"categories"`
    Packages   map[string]Package   `json:"packages"`
}
type Category struct {
    Label    string   `json:"label"`
    OSFilter []string `json:"os_filter,omitempty"`
    Packages []string `json:"packages"`
}
type Package struct {
    Description string                    `json:"description"`
    Binary      string                    `json:"binary,omitempty"`
    StowDir     string                    `json:"stow_dir,omitempty"`
    NoFolding   bool                      `json:"no_folding,omitempty"`
    ConfigOnly  bool                      `json:"config_only,omitempty"`
    Install     map[string]InstallMethod  `json:"install,omitempty"`
}
type InstallMethod struct {
    Brew     string `json:"brew,omitempty"`
    Pacman   string `json:"pacman,omitempty"`
    AUR      string `json:"aur,omitempty"`
    Apt      string `json:"apt,omitempty"`
    Npm      string `json:"npm,omitempty"`
    Corepack string `json:"corepack,omitempty"`
    Native   string `json:"native,omitempty"`
    Manual   string `json:"manual,omitempty"`
}
```

## Functions
- `LoadManifest(path string) (*Manifest, error)` — parse + validate
- `PackagesForPlatform(m *Manifest, distro string) map[string]Package` — filter by OS
- `ResolveInstallMethod(pkg Package, distro string) (*InstallMethod, error)` — platform-specific > "all" > error

## Resolution Chain
Install method resolution: check distro key first (e.g. "arch"), then OS key ("macos"), then "all". Missing = skip on this platform.

## packages.json Structure
7 categories, 47 packages. Read `installer/config.sh` for the CANONICAL source of truth:
- Category arrays: MACOS_PKGS, SHELL_PKGS, DEV_PKGS, AI_PKGS, FILE_PKGS, GIT_PKGS, SYSTEM_PKGS
- Brew mappings: `get_brew_pkg()` — e.g. nvim→neovim, claude→claude-code, ghostty→"--cask ghostty"
- Pacman mappings: `get_pacman_pkg()` — e.g. gh→github-cli, AUR prefixed with "aur:"
- Apt mappings: `get_apt_pkg()` — e.g. fd→fd-find, python→python3
- Binary names: `get_binary_name()` — e.g. ripgrep→rg, wireguard-tools→wg
- Alt methods: `get_alt_install_method()` — npm:prettier, corepack:pnpm, native scripts
- No-folding packages: nvim, zsh, ghostty, claude

Key packages with `Native` install (Go function names, NOT shell strings):
- nvim on debian: "InstallNvimTarball"
- ghostty on debian: "InstallGhosttyAppImage"
- starship on debian: "InstallStarshipScript"
- claude on non-macOS: "InstallClaudeCLI"
- node on debian: "InstallNodeNodesource"
- localsend on debian: "InstallLocalsendLinux"

Config-only packages (no binary, just stow): themes

## Verification
- `go test ./internal/manifest/...` passes
- Test: parse packages.json, verify all 47 packages present
- Test: category OSFilter works (macos category hidden on linux)
- Test: ResolveInstallMethod prefers platform-specific over "all"
- Test: package with no method for current platform returns error
- Test: validate catches missing description, invalid category references

## Reference Code
- Bash package definitions (CANONICAL): `~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles/installer/config.sh`
- Steez manifest pattern: `~/Projects/Personal/steez/internal/installer/manifest.go`

## Context
Bead 2 of 7. Module: `github.com/st-eez/dotfiles`. go.mod should already exist from bead 1. If not, create it.

title: Go installer: scaffold + platform detection + config persistence
description:
## What
Create the Go project foundation for the dotfiles installer migration (dot-6mp).

## Files to Create
- `go.mod` — module `github.com/st-eez/dotfiles`, Go 1.24.2
- `cmd/dotfiles/main.go` — CLI router with `version`, `help` commands. Use `flag.NewFlagSet` per command (same pattern as steez: `~/Projects/Personal/steez/cmd/steez/main.go`)
- `internal/platform/detect.go` + `detect_test.go` — OS/distro/Omarchy detection
- `internal/config/config.go` + `config_test.go` — JSON persistence at `~/.config/dotfiles/`
- `Makefile` — `build`, `test`, `clean` targets
- Update `.gitignore` — add compiled `dotfiles` binary

## Platform Detection (internal/platform/detect.go)
```go
type Info struct {
    OS        string // "macos", "linux"
    Distro    string // "macos", "arch", "debian"
    Arch      string // "arm64", "amd64"
    IsOmarchy bool
    Home      string
}
func Detect() (Info, error)
```
- macOS: `runtime.GOOS == "darwin"`
- Linux distros: parse `/etc/os-release` ID field. arch/endeavouros/manjaro/garuda → "arch". ubuntu/debian/pop/linuxmint → "debian". Fallback: "unknown"
- Omarchy: `~/.local/share/omarchy/bin` directory exists
- Arch: `runtime.GOARCH` mapped to "arm64"/"amd64"

## Config Persistence (internal/config/config.go)
```go
type Config struct {
    RepoPath     string `json:"repo_path"`
    FirstInstall string `json:"first_install"`
    LastRun      string `json:"last_run"`
}
type Registry struct {
    Packages []InstalledPackage `json:"packages"`
}
type InstalledPackage struct {
    Name        string `json:"name"`
    BinaryOK    bool   `json:"binary_ok"`
    ConfigOK    bool   `json:"config_ok"`
    InstalledAt string `json:"installed_at"`
    Method      string `json:"method"`
}
```
Config dir: `~/.config/dotfiles/`. Files: `config.json`, `installed.json`.
Follow steez pattern: `~/Projects/Personal/steez/internal/config/config.go`

## CLI Router (cmd/dotfiles/main.go)
Commands: `setup` (placeholder), `install` (placeholder), `version`, `help`.
Version: `3.0.0` (major bump from bash 2.1.0).
Pattern: read `~/Projects/Personal/steez/cmd/steez/main.go` for the `flag.NewFlagSet` + switch dispatch pattern.

## Verification

- `./dotfiles help` prints usage
- `go test ./internal/platform/... ./internal/config/...` passes

- Steez config: `~/Projects/Personal/steez/internal/config/config.go`

The repo is at `~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles` — Go source coexists alongside stow packages at the repo root. title: Move netsuite-browse skill to steez repo + narrow install path
description:
## Context
The browse binary (steez/skills/browse/) has a NetSuite protocol layer (ns commands) built in. The companion netsuite-browse SKILL.md teaches Claude how to use these commands. Currently the skill lives in the private NetSuite monorepo but the binary lives in the public steez repo. They're one logical unit split across two repos.

## Codex recommendation (2026-03-31)
Treat netsuite-browse as part of the public API surface of browse. Publish them together from steez. Let the private monorepo consume that published artifact instead of owning a second copy.

## Scope

### 1. Move skill to steez
- Copy netsuite-browse SKILL.md from monorepo to steez/skills/netsuite-browse/
- Add to skills.json under infrastructure category with requires: browse-binary
- Add steez preamble (coordinate with dot-dih)

### 2. Narrow install path for coworkers
- steez install browse should install browse + netsuite-browse together (they're one product)
- Or: standalone install script that doesn't require full steez CLI

### 3. Monorepo consumer script
- Add scripts/install-netsuite-browse.sh to monorepo
- Fetches latest browse binary + netsuite-browse SKILL.md from steez
- Coworkers run this instead of installing full steez

### 4. Remove old copy from monorepo
- Delete .claude/skills/netsuite-browse/ from monorepo
- Update monorepo docs to point to steez

## Key decisions
- netsuite-browse is generic (works for any NetSuite instance), so it belongs in the public repo
- Binary and skill should be versioned together
- Monorepo becomes a consumer, not an owner

title: steez installer: migration — run on own machine + update dotfiles
description:
# Migration — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite that has been extracted into a standalone repo (~/Projects/Personal/steez) with a Go+Bubble Tea installer. All code is built and tested. This bead executes the actual migration on the author's machine and updates the dotfiles repo.

## Prerequisites
- Beads 1-9 all complete
- steez repo at ~/Projects/Personal/steez with all skills, working CLI, passing tests
- `steez` binary installed via `go install` and available in PATH

## Scope

### Step 1: Verify steez repo is ready
```bash
cd ~/Projects/Personal/steez
go test ./internal/...    # all tests pass
steez version             # prints version
steez install starter --dry-run  # shows planned symlinks
```

### Step 2: Break the stow fold
```bash
# Current state: ~/.claude/skills/ is a symlink → dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/
# Verify:
ls -la ~/.claude/skills/  # should show symlink

# Break the fold:
unlink ~/.claude/skills/
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/

# Re-stow claude package with --no-folding:
stow --dir="$HOME/Projects/Personal/dotfiles" --target="$HOME" --no-folding --restow claude

# Verify: ~/.claude/skills/ is now a real directory with individual symlinks for non-steez content
ls -la ~/.claude/skills/
```

### Step 3: Install steez
```bash
steez setup  # or: steez install starter
# Verify:
steez doctor  # all checks pass
steez list    # shows installed skills
# Test a skill:
# invoke /steez-office-hours in Claude Code and verify it works
```

### Step 4: Update dotfiles repo
In ~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles:

1. **Update CLAUDE.md** — change the --no-folding rule:
   - FROM: "Do NOT use `--no-folding` for the `claude` package"
   - TO: "MUST use `--no-folding` for the `claude` package — steez symlinks require a real directory, not a stow-folded symlink"

2. **Update installer/install.sh** — add --no-folding for claude package in stow_package():
   - Add "claude" to the list alongside nvim, zsh, ghostty that use --no-folding

3. **Remove steez skills from dotfiles:**
   ```bash
   rm -rf claude/.claude/skills/steez/
   # Keep non-steez skills: agenda, audit, claude-spawn, jira, reminders, tmux
   # Keep non-skill content: CLAUDE.md (stowed to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md)
   ```

4. **Update .stow-local-ignore if needed**

5. **Re-stow claude package to verify:**
   ```bash
   stow --dir="$HOME/Projects/Personal/dotfiles" --target="$HOME" --no-folding --simulate --verbose --restow claude
   # Should show: only CLAUDE.md, hooks/, statusline.sh, and non-steez skills being linked
   stow --dir="$HOME/Projects/Personal/dotfiles" --target="$HOME" --no-folding --restow claude
   ```

### Step 5: Verify everything works
```bash
# steez skills still work:
steez doctor  # all pass

# dotfiles non-steez skills still work:
ls -la ~/.claude/skills/  # should show steez-* symlinks AND non-steez skill dirs

# Claude Code can discover all skills:
# Open Claude Code, verify /steez-office-hours and /agenda both appear
```

### Step 6: Commit and push both repos
```bash
# Dotfiles repo:
cd ~/Projects/Personal/dotfiles
git add -A
git commit -m "refactor: remove steez skills, use --no-folding for claude package

steez skills now live in standalone repo (st-eez/steez).
Installed via 'steez setup' with symlinks to ~/.claude/skills/.
The claude stow package must use --no-folding so both stow and
steez can write symlinks into the real ~/.claude/skills/ directory."
git push

# Steez repo:
cd ~/Projects/Personal/steez
git push  # should already have commits from beads 1-9
```

### Rollback procedure (if anything breaks)
```bash
steez uninstall --all                 # remove all steez symlinks
rm -r ~/.claude/skills/               # remove the real directory
# In dotfiles repo: git revert the commit that removed steez skills
stow --dir="$HOME/Projects/Personal/dotfiles" --target="$HOME" --restow claude
# This restores the stow-folded layout
```

## Key decisions
- This is the one-way door. After pushing both repos, rollback requires git reverts.
- Test EVERYTHING before committing. Run steez doctor AND verify Claude Code skill discovery.
- The --no-folding change in install.sh is permanent — future stow operations on claude use it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ~/.claude/skills/ is a real directory (not a symlink)
- [ ] steez doctor passes with all installed skills
- [ ] Non-steez skills (agenda, jira, etc.) still work
- [ ] Claude Code discovers both steez and non-steez skills
- [ ] dotfiles repo no longer contains steez skill directories
- [ ] CLAUDE.md --no-folding rule is updated
- [ ] install.sh stow_package uses --no-folding for claude
- [ ] Both repos committed and pushed
- [ ] steez update works (git pull on steez repo)
close_reason: Migration complete. Stow fold broken, 33 skills installed via steez (25 original + 8 migrated non-steez). Dotfiles cleaned: only gstack remains. CLAUDE.md and install.sh updated for --no-folding. Both repos pushed.

title: steez installer: tests (27 unit + 3 integration)
description:
# Tests — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite in a standalone repo with a Go+Bubble Tea installer. This bead adds full test coverage for the core installer, migration detection, doctor, and updater.

## Scope
