title: steez installer: updater + browse build
description:
# Updater + Browse Build — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite in a standalone repo with a Go+Bubble Tea installer. This bead implements `steez update` (pull latest + re-symlink) and browse binary build orchestration.

## Scope

### internal/updater/update.go

Functions:
- `RunUpdate() error` — main update flow

Flow:
1. Read repo_path from ~/.steez/config. Validate it's a git repo containing skills.json.
2. Check for dirty working tree: `git -C $REPO_PATH status --porcelain`
   - If dirty: abort with "You have local modifications to skill files. Stash or commit them first: git -C $REPO_PATH stash"
   - Distinguish dirty from diverged in error messages
3. Save current HEAD hash: `git -C $REPO_PATH rev-parse HEAD`
4. Pull: `git -C $REPO_PATH pull --ff-only`
   - If ff-only fails (diverged): abort with "Local and remote have diverged. Resolve manually: git -C $REPO_PATH pull --rebase"
5. Compare new HEAD hash to saved hash
   - If same: "Already up to date." exit
6. Re-symlink: for each skill in installed.json, verify the symlink still resolves. Create symlinks for any new skills that the user's profile includes.
7. Check if Go source changed (any .go files in diff): if yes, rebuild binary
   - Build to temp file: `go build -o $REPO_PATH/steez.new ./cmd/steez`
   - Validate: `$REPO_PATH/steez.new version` succeeds
   - Atomic rename: `os.Rename("steez.new", "steez")`
   - Then re-install: `go install ./cmd/steez` (updates ~/go/bin/steez)
   - Print: "steez CLI updated. You're now running the new version."
8. Run doctor, report results

### internal/installer/browse.go

Functions:
- `BuildBrowse(repoPath string) error` — build the browse binary
- `CheckBunVersion(minVersion string) error` — verify Bun is installed and meets minimum

Flow:
1. Check Bun is installed: `which bun`
   - If not found: return error "Bun not installed. Install: curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash"
2. Check Bun version: `bun --version`
   - Compare against minimum version from skills/browse/package.json engines field (or hardcode 1.0.0 for v1)
   - If below minimum: return error "Bun X.Y.Z found, minimum X.Y.Z required"
3. Check for partial prior build: if skills/browse/node_modules/ exists but looks incomplete (no .package-lock.json or equivalent), wipe it: `rm -rf skills/browse/node_modules/`
4. Run: `cd skills/browse && bun install && bun run build`
   - Stream stdout/stderr to the user (for progress visibility)
5. Verify: skills/browse/dist/browse exists and is executable
   - If not: return error "Browse build failed. Check output above."

### Key decisions from review
- steez update does NOT overwrite the running binary in-place. It builds to temp, validates, then atomic renames. Then runs `go install` to update ~/go/bin/steez.
- Dirty working tree is detected BEFORE pull, not after
- Diverged history gets a different error message than dirty tree
- Browse build wipes partial node_modules before retry
- Bun version is checked before build
- Browse build streams output (not swallowed)

## Inputs
- internal/config/ (repo_path from config, installed.json from bead 3)
- internal/installer/symlink.go (from bead 3)
- internal/installer/doctor.go (from bead 5)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `steez update` on a clean repo: pulls, re-symlinks, reports results
- [ ] `steez update` on a dirty repo: aborts with clear message
- [ ] `steez update` on a diverged repo: aborts with different message
- [ ] Binary rebuild uses temp file + atomic rename (never overwrites running binary)
- [ ] Browse build checks Bun version before starting
- [ ] Browse build wipes partial node_modules
- [ ] Browse build streams output to terminal
close_reason: update.go: full update flow (dirty check, ff-only pull, re-symlink, atomic binary rebuild, doctor). browse.go: Bun check, streaming build, partial wipe. Wired into CLI. Pushed.

title: steez installer: TUI (Bubble Tea setup flow)
description:
# TUI — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite in a standalone repo with a Go+Bubble Tea TUI installer. This bead builds the interactive setup wizard using Bubble Tea and Lip Gloss.

## Scope
Create three files in internal/tui/:

### internal/tui/theme.go — Lip Gloss design tokens

Port the ANSI palette approach from dotfiles installer/theme.sh:
- Primary: blue (ANSI 4)
- Secondary: cyan (ANSI 6)
- Accent: magenta (ANSI 5)
- Success: green (ANSI 2)
- Warning: yellow (ANSI 3)
- Error: red (ANSI 1)
- Text: white (ANSI 7)
- Use Lip Gloss adaptive colors so they respect terminal themes
- Support NO_COLOR env var (plain text, no styling)

### internal/tui/picker.go — Skill multi-select

Bubble Tea model for the skill picker screen:
- Display skills grouped by category with category headers
- Each skill shows: [x] or [ ] checkbox, skill name, description (from manifest)
- Skills with unmet requirements show [!] with footer note: "Requires browse binary"
- Category headers are non-selectable, visually distinct
- Selection indicator: [x] selected, [ ] not selected, [!] disabled
- Guard: cannot proceed with 0 skills selected — show footer error
- Keybindings: j/k OR arrow keys to navigate, space to toggle, enter to confirm, q/esc to quit
- Minimum terminal width: 60 columns. Truncate descriptions at terminal_width - 20

### internal/tui/setup.go — Main TUI flow

Bubble Tea model orchestrating 6 steps:

**Step 1: Splash (orientation-first)**
- If returning user (check ~/.steez/config for first_install): skip splash, go to step 2
- Show: one-line value statement ("steez installs Claude Code workflow skills via symlinks to ~/.claude/skills/")
- Show: version, system info (OS, arch, shell)
- Press enter to continue

**Step 2: Profile Select**
- 3 options (NOT 4 — "Browse Binary Only" is removed, use --browse CLI flag):
  - Starter Kit (recommended) — "6 workflow skills: the sprint pipeline"
  - All Skills — "Everything except domain-specific"
  - Custom — "Pick exactly what you want"
- Arrow keys / j/k to select, enter to confirm

**Step 3: Skill Picker (Custom only)**
- If Starter Kit or All Skills selected, skip to step 4
- Show the multi-select picker (picker.go)
- Category-grouped, with descriptions

**Step 4: Migration Gate (Case A only)**
- If DetectMigration() returns StateStowFolded or StateParentFolded:
  - Show: what was detected ("~/.claude/skills/ is a stow symlink pointing to ...")
  - Show: exact commands to run (generated by migrate.go)
  - Show: "Run these commands, then press Enter to continue"
  - On enter: re-run DetectMigration() to verify the fold is broken
  - If still folded: show error "Migration not complete. Please run the commands above."
  - Loop until resolved or user quits
- If Case B or C: skip this step

**Step 5: Preflight**
- Show: what will be installed (list of skill names with symlink paths)
- Show: install location (~/.claude/skills/)
- Show: runtime dir (~/.steez/)
- If browse-dependent skills selected and no browse binary: note "Browse binary will be built (requires Bun)"
- Confirm: enter to proceed, q to cancel

**Step 6: Install + Summary**
- Create symlinks one by one with per-skill status:
  - "Installing steez-office-hours... done ✓"
  - "Installing steez-review... done ✓"
- If browse build needed: spinner with "Building browse binary... 47s"
- Run doctor after install
- Summary with 3 states:
  - **Full success:** "Installed 6 skills. All checks pass. Try: /steez-office-hours"
  - **Partial success:** "Installed 4 of 6 skills. 2 failed (see above). Run steez doctor for details."
  - **No-op:** "All selected skills already installed. Run steez list to see them."

### Dependencies
- github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea
- github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss
- github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles (for spinner, list components)
- Run: go get github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles

### Key decisions from review
- NO "Browse Binary Only" option in TUI (use --browse CLI flag instead)
- NO install scope step (always global for v1)
- Splash skipped for returning users
- Migration gate loops until resolved
- Both vim-style (j/k) and arrow keys work
- Min 60 cols terminal width

## Inputs
- internal/installer/ (symlink.go, manifest.go, migrate.go, doctor.go from beads 3-5)
- internal/config/ (from bead 3)
- skills.json (from bead 1)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `steez setup` launches a TUI that guides through all 6 steps
- [ ] Starter Kit profile installs 6 skills + shared home
- [ ] Custom profile shows category-grouped multi-select picker
- [ ] Migration gate detects Case A and shows correct commands
- [ ] NO_COLOR env var disables all styling
- [ ] j/k and arrow keys both work for navigation
- [ ] q/esc quits cleanly at any step
- [ ] Summary shows correct state (success/partial/no-op)
close_reason: TUI: theme.go (ANSI + NO_COLOR), picker.go (category multi-select with j/k+arrows), setup.go (6-step flow: splash/profile/picker/migration/preflight/install). Bubble Tea + Lipgloss deps. Wired into CLI. Build + vet pass. Pushed.

title: steez installer: CLI commands (main.go + all subcommands)
description:
# CLI Commands — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite in a standalone repo with a Go+Bubble Tea installer. This bead wires all subcommands into the main CLI entry point.

## Scope
Create cmd/steez/main.go with all subcommands. Use Go standard library (flag package) or cobra — implementer's choice based on complexity.

### Commands

**steez setup** — launch TUI (implemented in bead 7, this bead just wires it)
- No flags needed (TUI handles all choices interactively)
- Calls internal/tui.RunSetup()

**steez install <skills...>** — non-interactive install
- `steez install starter` — install Starter Kit profile
- `steez install all` — install all skills
- `steez install office-hours ship review` — install specific skills
- Flags: --dry-run (show what would be created), --force (overwrite existing symlinks), --browse (also build browse binary), --local (install to .claude/skills/ instead of ~/.claude/skills/)
- Always installs the shared home symlink (skills/steez/) alongside selected skills
- Registers all created symlinks in installed.json
- Prints summary: "Installed 6 skills. Run steez doctor to verify."
- Exit code 0 on success, 1 on error, 2 on partial success

**steez uninstall <skills...>** — remove symlinks
- `steez uninstall qa benchmark` — remove specific skills
- `steez uninstall --all` — remove ALL steez-managed symlinks (reads from installed.json)
- Only removes symlinks that are in the registry
- Updates installed.json after removal
- Prints summary: "Removed 2 skills."

**steez list** — show installed skills
- Read from installed.json
- Show: skill name, symlink path, status (valid/dangling)
- Format: table with columns

**steez info <skill>** — show skill details
- Read from skills.json manifest
- Show: name, category, description, requires (if any), installed status
- Fuzzy match: if skill name doesn't match, show "did you mean: X?"

**steez doctor** — validate install health (implemented in bead 5, this wires it)
- Flags: --fix (auto-repair what it can)
- Calls internal/installer.RunDoctor(fix)

**steez update** — update steez (implemented in bead 8, this wires it)
- Calls internal/updater.RunUpdate()

**steez version** — print version
- Print "steez v1.0.0" (hardcode for v1, embed via ldflags later)
- Also show: Go version, OS/arch, repo path from config

**steez help** — print usage
- List all commands with one-line descriptions
- `steez help <command>` for detailed help

### Global behavior
- If no subcommand: print help
- Unknown subcommand: "Unknown command 'foo'. Run steez help for usage."
- All commands that modify filesystem respect --dry-run
- All commands print to stdout, errors to stderr

### Key decisions
- --local flag is v1 scope but only matters for domain-specific skills (netsuite-browse). For v1, default is always global install (~/.claude/skills/). --local creates symlinks in the current directory's .claude/skills/ instead.
- --browse flag on install triggers Bun build of the browse binary after symlink creation
- steez install always creates the shared home symlink (skills/steez/) even if user didn't explicitly request it

## Inputs
- internal/installer/ (bead 3, 4, 5)
- internal/updater/ (bead 8)
- internal/tui/ (bead 7)
- internal/config/ (bead 3)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `go install ./cmd/steez` succeeds
- [ ] `steez version` prints version info
- [ ] `steez help` lists all commands
- [ ] `steez install starter --dry-run` shows planned symlinks without creating them
- [ ] `steez list` shows installed skills (or "No skills installed")
- [ ] `steez info office-hours` shows skill details from manifest
- [ ] `steez uninstall --all` removes all registered symlinks
- [ ] Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 2=partial
- [ ] Unknown commands show helpful error
close_reason: All 8 subcommands wired: install (with profiles + fuzzy match + migration gate + dry-run), uninstall, list, info, doctor, update (stub), setup (stub), version, help. Fixed relative symlink resolution bug in deriveStowPaths. Pushed.

title: steez installer: doctor (validate install health)
description:
# Doctor — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite in a standalone repo with a Go+Bubble Tea installer. steez doctor validates the health of an installation: symlinks resolve, runtime dirs exist, shared home is intact.

## Scope
Create internal/installer/doctor.go

### Functions
```go
type CheckResult struct {
    Name    string  // e.g. "steez-office-hours symlink"
    Status  string  // "pass", "fail", "warn"
    Message string  // human-readable detail
    FixCmd  string  // command to fix (for --fix mode), empty if n/a
}

func RunDoctor(fix bool) ([]CheckResult, error)
```

### Checks to run (in order)

1. **Shared home symlink** (FAIL-FAST)
   - ~/.claude/skills/steez/ must exist and be a valid symlink
   - If missing or broken, ALL skills are broken — fail immediately with clear message
   - Fix: `steez install` (re-creates shared home symlink)

2. **Runtime directory**
   - ~/.steez/ must exist
   - ~/.steez/config must exist
   - ~/.steez/analytics/ must exist
   - Fix: create missing dirs

3. **Registered symlinks** (from installed.json only)
   - For each symlink in the registry: does it exist? Does it resolve to a real directory?
   - Dangling symlink (target deleted): WARN with fix command
   - Missing symlink (removed manually): WARN with fix command
   - Fix (--fix mode): remove dangling symlinks from filesystem AND registry

4. **Non-registered steez symlinks** (detection only)
   - Scan ~/.claude/skills/steez-* for symlinks NOT in the registry
   - WARN: "Found unregistered steez symlink: steez-foo. Run steez install foo to register it."
   - Do NOT touch these (they might be manually managed)

5. **Browse binary** (only if browse-dependent skills are installed)
   - Check skills/browse/dist/browse exists and is executable
   - If missing: WARN "Browse binary not built. Run steez setup --browse"

### Output format
```
steez doctor
  ✓ Shared home symlink          ~/.claude/skills/steez/ → ~/Projects/Personal/steez/skills/steez/
  ✓ Runtime directory             ~/.steez/
  ✓ steez-office-hours            ~/.claude/skills/steez-office-hours/ → .../skills/office-hours/
  ✓ steez-review                  ~/.claude/skills/steez-review/ → .../skills/review/
  ✗ steez-qa                      DANGLING — target directory missing
  ! Browse binary                 Not built. Run: steez setup --browse

5 checks passed, 1 failed, 1 warning
```

### --fix mode
When --fix is passed, doctor auto-repairs what it can:
- Create missing dirs (runtime, analytics)
- Remove dangling symlinks from filesystem + registry
- Print what it fixed: "Fixed: removed dangling symlink steez-qa"
- Do NOT create missing symlinks (that's steez install's job)
- Do NOT build browse binary (that requires Bun, user must opt in)

### Exit codes
- 0: all checks pass
- 1: any check failed
- 2: warnings only (no failures)

## Inputs
- internal/config/config.go (LoadRegistry from bead 3)
- internal/installer/symlink.go (ValidateSymlink from bead 3)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Doctor checks shared home first and fails fast if broken
- [ ] Only checks symlinks in the registry (doesn't touch non-steez content)
- [ ] Detects unregistered steez-* symlinks as warnings
- [ ] --fix removes dangling symlinks
- [ ] Exit codes match spec (0/1/2)
- [ ] Output is structured and human-readable
close_reason: doctor.go: 5 check categories, --fix mode, exit codes 0/1/2. Build + vet pass. Pushed.

title: steez installer: migration detection (Case A/B/C)
description:
# Migration Detection — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite in a standalone repo with a Go+Bubble Tea installer. Before creating symlinks, the installer must detect the current state of ~/.claude/skills/ and handle migration from the dotfiles stow-folded layout.

## Background
Currently, ~/.claude/skills/ is a single stow-folded symlink pointing at dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/. The steez installer needs it to be a real directory containing individual symlinks. The user must break the fold before steez can install.

## Scope
Create internal/installer/migrate.go

### Cases to detect

**Case A: ~/.claude/skills/ is a stow-folded symlink**
- This is the current state for existing users
- Detected by: os.Lstat shows symlink, resolves to a path inside a dotfiles repo
- Action: the TUI shows a migration gate screen with commands to run

**Case A2: ~/.claude/ itself is a symlink (parent folded)**
- Stow sometimes folds parent directories too
- Detected by: stat each component of the path (~/.claude, ~/.claude/skills)
- Action: guide user to unlink the parent first, then handle the child

**Case B: ~/.claude/skills/ is a real directory**
- Clean state. May contain non-steez skills. No migration needed.
- Detected by: os.Lstat shows regular directory (not symlink)
- Action: proceed with install, create steez symlinks alongside existing content

**Case C: ~/.claude/skills/ does not exist**
- Fresh machine or Claude Code not yet configured
- Detected by: os.Stat returns os.ErrNotExist
- Action: create the directory, proceed with install

### Functions
```go
type MigrationState int
const (
    StateStowFolded     MigrationState = iota  // Case A
    StateParentFolded                           // Case A2
    StateRealDirectory                          // Case B
    StateMissing                                // Case C
)

type MigrationResult struct {
    State       MigrationState
    SymlinkPath string  // resolved symlink target (Case A/A2 only)
    Commands    []string // shell commands user needs to run (Case A/A2 only)
}

func DetectMigration() (*MigrationResult, error)
```

### Migration commands generated for Case A:
```bash
unlink ~/.claude/skills/
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/
stow --dir="$HOME/Projects/Personal/dotfiles" --target="$HOME" --no-folding --restow claude
```

For Case A2, prepend:
```bash
unlink ~/.claude/
mkdir -p ~/.claude/
# then the Case A commands
```

### Key decisions
- Detection stats EACH path component, not just the leaf
- The installer does NOT run these commands automatically (manual gate screen)
- Commands are generated with the actual resolved paths, not hardcoded
- The stow --dir path is derived from resolving the existing symlink target

## Inputs
- internal/installer/symlink.go (IsSymlink function from bead 3)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] DetectMigration() correctly identifies all 4 cases
- [ ] Case A generates correct unlink + mkdir + stow commands
- [ ] Case A2 detects parent-level symlinks
- [ ] Case B returns StateRealDirectory with no commands
- [ ] Case C returns StateMissing with no commands
- [ ] No hardcoded paths — derives dotfiles location from symlink resolution
close_reason: migrate.go implements DetectMigration() with all 4 cases. Derives stow paths from symlink resolution. Build + vet pass. Pushed.

title: steez installer: core installer (symlink + manifest + config)
description:
# Core Installer — steez standalone installer

## Context
steez is a 25-skill Claude Code suite in a standalone repo (~/Projects/Personal/steez) with a Go+Bubble Tea TUI installer. This bead implements the core installer logic: symlink management, manifest parsing, and config storage.

## Scope
Create three Go files in internal/installer/ and internal/config/:

### internal/installer/symlink.go

Functions:
- `CreateSymlink(source, target string, dryRun bool, force bool) error`
  - If dryRun: print "Would create symlink: target → source" and return nil
  - If target doesn't exist: create symlink, return nil
  - If target exists and is same symlink: return nil (idempotent)
  - If target exists and is different symlink: if force, remove and recreate; else return ErrSymlinkExists with guidance
  - If target exists and is a regular file/dir: return ErrTargetExists
  - If source dir doesn't exist: return ErrSourceMissing
  - If permission denied: return ErrPermission with the path
- `RemoveSymlink(target string) error`
  - If target is a symlink: remove it, return nil
  - If target is a real dir/file: return ErrNotSymlink (refuse to delete real content)
  - If target doesn't exist: return nil (idempotent)
- `IsSymlink(path string) (bool, string, error)` — returns (isSymlink, resolvedTarget, error)
- `ValidateSymlink(target string) error` — checks symlink exists AND resolves to a real directory

Error types: ErrSymlinkExists, ErrTargetExists, ErrSourceMissing, ErrPermission, ErrNotSymlink

### internal/installer/manifest.go

Functions:
- `LoadManifest(path string) (*Manifest, error)` — parse skills.json
  - Validate: every skill in every category has a description
  - Validate: every profile references existing categories
  - Validate: every skill with "requires" field references valid capabilities
  - Return structured error on invalid JSON, missing file, or validation failure
- `ResolveProfile(manifest *Manifest, profileName string) ([]string, error)` — returns skill names for a profile
  - "starter" → the 6 workflow skills
  - "all" → all skills minus excluded categories
  - Unknown profile → error with available profiles listed
- `FindSkill(manifest *Manifest, name string) (*Skill, error)` — fuzzy match
  - Exact match first
  - Levenshtein distance ≤ 2: return "did you mean: X?"
  - No match: return full skill list

Types:
```go
type Manifest struct {
    Version    string                `json:"version"`
    Categories map[string]Category   `json:"categories"`
    Profiles   map[string]Profile    `json:"profiles"`
    SharedInfra SharedInfra          `json:"shared_infra"`
}
type Category struct {
    Label       string   `json:"label"`
    Description string   `json:"description"`
    Skills      []Skill  `json:"skills"`
}
type Skill struct {
    Name        string   `json:"name"`
    Description string   `json:"description"`
    Requires    []string `json:"requires,omitempty"`
}
type Profile struct {
    Label             string   `json:"label"`
    Description       string   `json:"description"`
    Categories        []string `json:"categories,omitempty"`
    ExcludeCategories []string `json:"exclude_categories,omitempty"`
}
type SharedInfra struct {
    Bin          []string `json:"bin"`
    RuntimeDir   string   `json:"runtime_dir"`
    BrowseBinary string   `json:"browse_binary"`
}
```

### internal/config/config.go

Functions:
- `Load() (*Config, error)` — read ~/.steez/config (key=value format, one per line)
- `Save(config *Config) error` — write ~/.steez/config
- `EnsureDir() error` — create ~/.steez/ if missing
- `LoadRegistry() (*Registry, error)` — read ~/.steez/installed.json
- `SaveRegistry(reg *Registry) error` — write ~/.steez/installed.json
- `AddToRegistry(reg *Registry, name, source, target string) *Registry`
- `RemoveFromRegistry(reg *Registry, name string) *Registry`

Types:
```go
type Config struct {
    RepoPath     string `json:"repo_path"`
    FirstInstall string `json:"first_install"`
}
type Registry struct {
    Symlinks []RegisteredSymlink `json:"symlinks"`
}
type RegisteredSymlink struct {
    Name   string `json:"name"`    // e.g. "steez-office-hours"
    Source string `json:"source"`  // repo path
    Target string `json:"target"`  // ~/.claude/skills/steez-office-hours
}
```

### Key decisions
- installed.json tracks which symlinks steez created (prevents touching non-steez symlinks)
- Fuzzy match uses Levenshtein distance ≤ 2, max 3 suggestions
- All paths use $HOME expansion, never hardcoded absolute paths
- dryRun and force flags thread through from CLI layer

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `go build ./internal/...` compiles without errors
- [ ] Types are exported and documented with comments
- [ ] Error types are distinct and testable
- [ ] No hardcoded paths (use os.UserHomeDir())
close_reason: symlink.go, manifest.go, config.go implemented. Build + vet pass. Smoke test confirms 25 skills parse correctly. Pushed.

Decision: TIERED. Three tiers of voice porting:

description:
# Epic: Extract steez into standalone repo with Go CLI installer

## What
Extract 25 steez skills from dotfiles repo into standalone repo (~/Projects/Personal/steez/) with a Go+Bubble Tea TUI installer. Selective install via symlinks, starter kit profiles, doctor validation, git-based updates.

## Key Decisions (from /steez-autoplan review, 2026-03-30)
- **Toolchain:** Go + Bubble Tea (zero-dep binary, pattern ports to dot-6mp)
- **Distribution:** Makefile + `go install` (no binary in repo). Binary goes to ~/go/bin/steez
- **Install model:** Symlinks from ~/.claude/skills/steez-{name} → repo/skills/{name}/
- **Update model:** Live mutation (git pull in-place, symlinks already point at checkout)
- **Migration:** Manual gate screen in TUI (detects stow fold, shows commands, user runs them)
- **Skill descriptions:** Hand-written in skills.json manifest (80-char max)
- **Shared home:** skills/steez/ (meta-skill + bin/ helpers, dot-amv)
- **Install registry:** ~/.steez/installed.json tracks steez-managed symlinks
- **TUI changes from review:** Remove "Browse Binary Only" (use --browse flag), skip install scope for v1, add Case A migration gate, 3 summary states, orientation-first splash

## Design docs
- Design doc: ~/.steez/projects/st-eez-dotfiles/stevedimakos-main-design-20260330-204342.md
- Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/nifty-puzzling-pancake.md
- Test plan: ~/.steez/projects/st-eez-dotfiles/stevedimakos-main-eng-review-test-plan-20260330-222500.md

## Subtask ordering
1. Repo scaffold (go.mod, Makefile, skills.json, CLAUDE.md)
2. Move skills (copy 25 skill dirs from dotfiles)
3. Core installer (symlink.go, manifest.go, config.go)
4. Migration detection (migrate.go)
5. Doctor (doctor.go)
6. CLI commands (main.go with all subcommands)
7. TUI (Bubble Tea setup flow)
8. Updater + browse build
9. Tests (27 unit + 3 integration)
10. Migration (run on own machine, update dotfiles)

description:
Port review quality upgrades from gstack to steez. Three areas:

1. CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION (steez-review, steez-ship, steez-plan-eng-review, steez-cso): The full confidence system is ABSENT from steez-review (not reduced — completely removed per Codex spot-check). title: steez-docs-punchup — Rewrite README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md with narrative energy
description:
Rewrite steez/README.md and steez/ARCHITECTURE.md to match gstack's narrative documentation style. The current versions are accurate reference docs but lack the storytelling energy that makes gstack's docs effective.

## Why this matters
These docs are NOT just for humans. Skills actively read them at runtime:
- /steez-review cross-references diff against README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md for doc drift
- /steez-ship checks if docs changed in the PR
- /steez-document-release reads ALL docs and updates them to match what shipped
- /steez-design-consultation reads first 50 lines of README.md for project context

Richer docs = better skill context = better AI judgment.

## How to find the patterns
1. Read gstack's README: ~/.claude/skills/gstack/README.md (287 lines)
   - Opens with a hook (Karpathy quote, personal story)
   - "See it work" section — full conversation example showing the pipeline
   - "The sprint" — Think → Plan → Build → Review → Test → Ship → Reflect narrative
   - Skill table with "Your specialist" role metaphors
   - Troubleshooting section

2. Read gstack's ARCHITECTURE: ~/.claude/skills/gstack/ARCHITECTURE.md (~400 lines)
   - Opens with "the core idea" — one paragraph thesis
   - Deep "Why X" sections (Why Bun, Why Locators not DOM mutation)
   - ASCII diagrams (CLI → Server → Chromium)
   - Error philosophy section
   - "What's intentionally not here" section

3. Compare with current steez versions: claude/.claude/skills/steez/README.md, claude/.claude/skills/steez/ARCHITECTURE.md

## What to add to steez README
- Opening hook / thesis statement (not Garry's story — steez's identity)
- "See it work" conversation example showing the steez pipeline in action
- Pipeline explained as a workflow story, not just an ASCII diagram
- Skill table with persona/role energy (not just "what it does")
- The full 21-skill catalog (was 6 when first written)

## What to add to steez ARCHITECTURE
- "The core idea" thesis paragraph
- "Why" sections for key decisions:
  - Why no templates (5 skills manageable by hand, no build step, tradeoff is manual shared-section updates)
  - Why hardcoded solo (personal tooling, no team scenario)
  - Why local-only telemetry (no phone-home, still have JSONL analytics)
  - Why no onboarding (single user, config pre-seeded)
  - Why Skill Self-Report always on (you're the maintainer)
- "What's intentionally not here" section
- Error/failure philosophy for skills

## Key decisions (user-confirmed)
- No templates: 21 skills now (was 5 when decided), but still manageable
- Hardcoded solo: personal tooling
- Local-only telemetry: usage tracking via ~/.steez/analytics/skill-usage.jsonl
- No onboarding: config pre-seeded at ~/.steez/config
- Skill Self-Report always on: you're the maintainer

## Also update
- FORK_MANIFEST.md — add the 15 new skills to the Skills table
- README skill count — now 21 total, not 6

title: steez-lint — Grep for leftover gstack references
description:
After all 5 skills are ported, run a comprehensive grep to catch any leftover gstack references.

## Design Doc
~/.gstack/projects/scratchpad/stevedimakos-unknown-design-20260328-152755.md
See: Success Criteria — "No leftover /gstack-* or ~/.gstack/ references"

## What to grep

Run against all files in dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez* (skills + bin + assets):

```bash
# Skill chaining refs that should be steez-*
grep -rn "/gstack-" dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez*/

# Data directory refs that should be ~/.steez/
grep -rn "\.gstack/" dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez*/

# Binary path refs that should be steez/bin/steez-*
grep -rn "gstack/bin/" dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez*/

# Script names that should be steez-*
grep -rn "gstack-config\|gstack-slug\|gstack-review-log\|gstack-review-read\|gstack-diff-scope\|gstack-repo-mode" dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez*/

# Browse binary refs that should be steez browse
grep -rn "gstack-browse\|gstack/browse/dist" dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez*/

# ETHOS.md ref that should point to steez/
grep -rn "gstack/ETHOS" dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez*/

# remote-slug refs that should be steez-slug
grep -rn "remote-slug" dotfiles/claude/.claude/skills/steez*/
```

## Acceptable exceptions
- FORK_MANIFEST.md referencing gstack as upstream source — this is intentional
- The word "gstack" in prose context like "forked from gstack" — acceptable
- Comments explaining the fork origin — acceptable

## NOT acceptable
- Any executable path pointing to ~/.claude/skills/gstack/
- Any data path pointing to ~/.gstack/
- Any skill chaining ref like /gstack-office-hours
- Any binary name like gstack-config in a bash block

## Verification
Every grep above returns 0 matches (excluding acceptable exceptions).
close_reason: All greps clean. Fixed 2 remote-slug refs in greptile-triage.md. Only remaining gstack mentions are Phase 6 YC prose (non-functional).

title: Sketchybar quality simplifications: timer tick, calendar os.date, icon centralization, wake handler
description:
Audit quality cluster — small DRY/consistency improvements.

REQUIRED:
1. items/timer.lua:200-223 — routine and system_woke handlers are byte-identical. Extract a local tick() function; subscribe both events to it. Net -8 lines.

2. items/calendar.lua:27-41 — replace the manual 12h conversion with a single os.date("%a %b %-d %I:%M%p"). Net ~12 lines deleted. (%-d supported on macOS for non-padded day.)

3. items/spaces.lua:194 — separator chevron '􀆊' literal belongs in icons.lua alongside other glyphs. Add icons.separator = '􀆊' and reference.

4. items/spaces.lua — add system_woke handler to spaces_observer that re-runs update_windows(current_workspace). Window icon cache may be stale after wake if apps were quit while asleep. Other items (calendar, status_icons, timer) already handle system_woke.

OPTIONAL (lower confidence, skip if it complicates reload semantics):
5. init.lua:74 — sbar.exec("sketchybar --hotload true") runs after all item requires. Moving it to sketchybarrc (before require("init")) would make hotload semantics established earlier, but the current ordering works fine in practice. Defer unless the other changes happen to touch this area.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Reload works cleanly.
2. Timer behaves identically on wake and routine tick (same display, same countdown).
3. Clock still renders 'Fri Apr 18 3:45PM' shape.
4. Separator still renders between spaces and front_app.
5. Sleep the machine for 2+ minutes while a workspace has apps; wake and verify the app icons on that workspace are accurate (not stale).
close_reason:
4 of 5 items landed:
1. timer.lua — extracted shared tick() for routine + system_woke (-8 lines).
2. calendar.lua — collapsed to os.date one-liner with gsub to strip leading zeros (Lua 5.5 rejects GNU %-d/%-I). Verified live: 'Fri Apr 17 8:11PM'.
3. icons.lua — added separator = '􀆊'; spaces.lua now references icons.separator.
4. spaces.lua — added system_woke handler on spaces_observer that clears window_cache and re-runs update_windows for all spaces.
5. (optional) init.lua hotload move — skipped per bead description ('Defer unless the other changes happen to touch this area'). Not touched.
Verified: luac -p clean, sketchybar --reload exit 0, calendar label renders in expected format.

title: Clean up dead code and hard-coded constants in spaces.lua
description:
Audit cleanup cluster — small, independent fixes grouped for one sweep.

1. items/spaces.lua:7 — delete 'package.path = package.path .. ";./helpers/?.lua"'. Relative, cwd-dependent. Dead: the next line's require("helpers.icon_map") already resolves via the config root.

2. items/spaces.lua:299-315 — fetch_monitor_workspaces hard-codes 'if mon_idx > 3'. Derive upper bound from #monitor_list (already computed at :107-112). 4th display would otherwise be silently dropped.

3. items/spaces.lua:207-219 — front_app table has broken indentation. The 'font' table sits inside 'label' only by accident of Lua parsing. Currently correct; one careless edit from silently losing the font. Reformat with consistent 2-space indent.

4. items/spaces.lua:66-68 — update_highlight's icon_font ternary has both branches sharing size=16.0; only style differs. Simplify.

5. items/apple.lua:3 — delete unused 'local settings = require("settings")'.

6. items/spaces.lua:236-238 — Mission Control click handler uses 'open -a "Mission Control"'. This works on current macOS in my testing (exit 0, Mission Control triggered), but Codex reports failure in its environment and using the absolute app path is strictly more defensive. Change to:
    sbar.exec("open -a '/System/Applications/Mission Control.app'")
Zero downside.
acceptance_criteria:
1. sketchybar --reload succeeds with no errors.
2. Workspace switching still renders correctly.
3. front_app label still shows app name after switching apps.
4. Apple icon still renders.
5. Clicking front_app opens Mission Control.
6. With a single display attached, 5 workspaces show (not 10).
close_reason:
All 6 cleanup items landed:
1. Dead package.path append removed at spaces.lua.
2. mon_idx > 3 replaced with mon_idx > #monitor_list.
3. front_app table re-indented (consistent 2-space).
4. icon_font ternary collapsed — one table literal, size extracted.
5. Unused 'settings' require removed from apple.lua.
6. Mission Control handler now uses absolute path /System/Applications/Mission Control.app.
Verified: luac -p clean, sketchybar --reload exit 0.

title: Make sketchybarrc entrypoint cwd-independent and use $HOME
description: sketchybarrc:3 hardcodes /Users/$USER in package.cpath and sketchybarrc:5 uses require("init") which depends on Lua's default ./?.lua resolution (cwd-dependent). Normal sketchybar launches set cwd to the config dir so it works in practice, but smoke tests and alternate invocations (e.g. 'lua sketchybarrc' from /tmp, or running from a worktree) fail with 'module init not found'. Also, using $HOME alone resolves modules from the live symlinked config, so running from a worktree would still load the stowed files — not the worktree's. Wrong for testing changes.
design:
Derive config dir from the script's own path using debug.getinfo — this makes the entrypoint correct whether running live, from a worktree, or from a smoke test.

  #!/usr/bin/env lua
  local home = os.getenv("HOME") or ""
  local script = debug.getinfo(1, "S").source:sub(2)   -- strip leading '@'
  local config_dir = script:match("(.*)/") or "."
  package.path  = config_dir .. "/?.lua;"
              .. config_dir .. "/?/init.lua;"
              .. package.path
  package.cpath = home .. "/.local/share/sketchybar_lua/?.so;"
              .. package.cpath
  require("init")
  local sbar = require("sketchybar")
  sbar.event_loop()

The cpath still uses $HOME because sketchybar_lua's .so is installed into the user's home, not the config dir.
acceptance_criteria:
1. 'lua ~/.config/sketchybar/sketchybarrc' from /tmp resolves require("init") without error.
2. 'lua <worktree-path>/sketchybar/.config/sketchybar/sketchybarrc' loads modules from the worktree, NOT the live $HOME/.config/sketchybar symlink — verify by adding a trace print to a worktree items/*.lua and confirming it appears.
3. Normal 'sketchybar --reload' still works unchanged.
close_reason: sketchybarrc now derives config_dir from debug.getinfo script path, with $HOME/.config/sketchybar as fallback for colors.lua (theme-injected, only exists at stow target). Verified: luac clean, sketchybar --reload exit 0, module resolution from /tmp shows worktree modules load from script-dir, colors falls through to live as designed.

title: [bug] fix: browser disconnect handler bypasses shared cleanup
description: browser-manager.ts — browser 'disconnected' event handler calls process.exit(1) directly, bypassing server.ts shutdown() entirely. This means Chrome child processes, agent subprocesses, session state, and buffer flushes are all skipped. This is a separate leak vector from dot-18y (emergencyCleanup). Fix: route the disconnect handler through the shared shutdown() path, or at minimum call browserManager.close() before exiting.
close_reason: Merged into dot-18y — same root cause (exit paths bypassing browser cleanup). dot-18y description already updated to cover both vectors.

title: [bug] fix: 'is' assertions timeout on non-existent CSS selectors
description: read-commands.ts:254 — 'browse is enabled "#nonexistent"' hangs for the full Playwright timeout (~30s) instead of returning a fast 'element not found' error. Root cause: resolveRef() only does existence checks for @e/@c refs, not CSS selectors. For CSS, the first real DOM lookup happens inside Playwright's isEnabled()/isDisabled()/isChecked()/isEditable() which use auto-waiting. isVisible() is unaffected (Playwright special-cases it to return false immediately). Fix: add explicit locator.count() existence check before calling assertion methods that require an element. Keep visible/hidden as-is.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: emergencyCleanup skips browserManager.close()
description: server.ts:792 — emergencyCleanup() on uncaughtException/unhandledRejection doesn't close the browser. Normal shutdown() does at line 766. Child Chrome process can be reparented instead of killed. Fix: add try { await browserManager.close() } catch {} to emergencyCleanup. Note: this is necessary but not sufficient for full orphan prevention. See also: (1) browser disconnect handler bypasses cleanup entirely (separate bead), (2) SIGKILL/hard OOM can't run any JS cleanup, so startup-time orphan detection via SingletonLock is also needed, (3) cli.ts has partial orphan recovery but only in headed connect flow, not general daemon startup.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: unbounded chatBuffer grows forever in sidebar sessions
description: server.ts:131 — chatBuffer is a plain array with no cap. Streaming agent events (text_delta, tool_use, result) append per chunk, growing faster than 1 entry per message. The JSONL file on disk also grows unbounded. Cap the array or use CircularBuffer like console/network/dialog buffers.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: res.body() buffers entire response just for size logging

value: Aerospace launches sketchybar once via after-startup-command in aerospace-{home,laptop,office}.toml — it does NOT supervise it. If sketchybar dies mid-session, aerospace won't restart it. sketchybar --reload is a signal to a running process, so it silently no-ops when the process is dead. Manual relaunch: nohup /opt/homebrew/opt/sketchybar/bin/sketchybar >/tmp/sketchybar.log 2>&1 & disown. If crashes recur, add a launchd KeepAlive plist or watchdog instead of relying on aerospace.
st-eez is the user's GitHub username (not an org). Steez repo lives at github.com/st-eez/steez, cloned to ~/Projects/Personal/steez. Go module path: github.com/st-eez/steez
